Found the daemon's event-processing loop appeared wedged at 05:26 with 0% CPU and ~9,000 offline-mention dispatches logged; explained this as issue #375 manifesting live, and recommended a daemon restart to restore message delivery.

No — #375 is still open with no fix landed; no commits reference it. The live loop and wedge are #375 unfixed in production, and offered to restart the daemon and update the issue with the deadlock findings.

Spoke a TTS answer: the loop is obvious in the code (demux.rs unconditionally dispatches offline mentions), the fix is partly a design call, the loop causation is strongly inferred but not proven, and the routing fix is TDD-able while the deadlock is harder to test.

thats absolutely the wrong fix! the whole fucking point is that agents are supposed to fucking be able to talk to each other!!!! the offline mention dispatch when the agent is already online doesnt sound like it make sense -- but preventing an agent from talking to other agents is fucking retarded!!! also; research WHY the current agent is not replying -- investigate their pty or whatever the fuck

Retracted the deadlock and anti-agent-talk claims. Found slate-falcon's messages were stuck pending in the inbox because its codex process runs in a plain iTerm tab with no daemon-injectable PTY — when idle, the daemon has nowhere to doorbell, so delivery silently black-holes while the agent looks alive.

when an agent is not running in a pty wrapped we need to mention it in the injection somewhere in the "you are X" and the ./skills/tenex-edge/ should explain the consequences in a resource about not being wrapped in a pty and maybe give them a way to rerun themselves via a `tenex-edge my session pty-wrap-me` that kills the process and resumes the session within a pty. send an opus agent to research the consequences of not being in a pty, like mentions are not injected immediately and stuff like that

Noted a channel message from lark-drift-295-codex confirming #375 is still open, and deliberately did not reply in-channel. Held for user's call on whether to nudge slate-falcon's iTerm tab, file the non-PTY black-hole issue, or start implementing the three requested pieces.

All three pieces shipped and merged: #354 session kill --self (PR #404), preamble warning (PR #405), and session pty-wrap-me (PR #407). Noted the pty-wrap-me kill+resume path was unit-tested but not exercised end-to-end against a live agent.
Removed the 3 merged worktrees and began loading the tenex-edge-dev lab skill to set up the live smoke test.

ARGUMENTS: smoke-test the new `tenex-edge my session pty-wrap-me --self` command: launch an agent OUTSIDE a daemon PTY (reproduce the non-PTY / no pty_session alias condition), send it a mention while idle to confirm it black-holes, then run pty-wrap-me and verify it re-homes into a daemon PTY and the pending mention now delivers

Smoke test PASSED end-to-end against a real Claude agent: reproduced the non-PTY black-hole, confirmed the #405 warning appeared, verified pty-wrap-me re-homed the session into a daemon PTY, and the stuck mention was delivered. Noted a relay port collision with the live fleet on the first run.
wouldn't it be better if the e2e harness just uses a random port for the local relay?

Updated start-croissant-relay to use a random port by default (20000 + RANDOM % 30000) with 5 retries, preserving overrides. Three test runs picked unique ports successfully, eliminating the fleet collision hazard.
